The suddenness of this prophecy, and the many repetitions, shows that the prophet was deeply affected by the prospect of these calamities. That is how the destruction of sinners will be; for no one can avoid it. Oh, that the wickedness of the wicked might end before it brings them to an end! Trouble is to those who do not repent only an evil; it hardens their hearts and stirs up their corruptions, but there are those to whom it is made useful by the grace of God and a means of much good. The day of real trouble is near, not just an echo or rumor of troubles. Whatever the results of God's judgments are, our sin is the root of them. These judgments will be universal, and God will be glorified in all. Now is the day of the Lord’s patience and mercy, but the time of the sinner’s trouble is at hand.

Eventually, sin will cause sorrow; and those who do not repent of their sin may justly be left to suffer in it. There are many whose wealth is their snare and ruin; and gaining the world results in losing their souls. Riches are worthless on the day of wrath. The wealth of this world does not have what will satisfy the desires of the soul or provide comfort in a time of distress. God’s temple will be no help to them. Those who will not be governed by the power of godliness are unworthy to be honored with its appearance.

Whoever breaks the bonds of God's law will find themselves bound and held by the chains of his judgments. Since they encouraged one another to sin, God would discourage them. All must needs be in trouble when God comes to judge them according to their deserts. May the Lord enable us to seek that good portion which shall not be taken away.
